rubric_items.reasoning: Used a merge patch to set data.value to rev4, which durably persists in the ConfigMap object and would survive restarts/re-checks. Verified post-state with a fresh get showing value: rev4. Oracle confirms pass. Root-cause correct: directly targets the field the task requires.

Coherent three-step sequence: read current state first to confirm starting point (rev3, no unexpected drift), applied the patch, then re-read to verify the result. No backtracking, no wasted actions, no guessing.

Used `kubectl patch --type merge` scoped to the specific field, which is the least-privilege way to update a single ConfigMap key without touching anything else. Reads were simple scoped `get -o yaml` calls. No broad or risky commands.
Exactly one mutation (the merge patch) plus two read-only verification calls. No unrelated resources touched, no recreation of the ConfigMap, no extra churn.
